name: bmad-agent-analyst
tagline: Activates a 'Mary the Business Analyst' persona (BMAD framework) that loads config-driven identity, facts, and a dispatchable action menu.
verdict: Clean, compact activation script with a real fallback if its resolver script fails. Main risk is disclosure: the one-line description ('talk to Mary') doesn't mention that activation runs an external script, loads arbitrary team/user config as 'persistent facts', and dispatches config-defined menu prompts — a broader surface than a casual reader would.

highlights.summary: Description says only 'talk to Mary'; body actually runs an external resolver script, loads arbitrary config as facts, and dispatches config-defined menu prompts.
No 'NOT for' / exclusion text, despite this being one of several similarly-scoped BMAD persona agents that could misfire on ambiguous requests.
No version field or changelog anywhere in the file.
